concerns: Step 5 contains a factual error: it claims fractalGrowthWireframe 'has no per-vertex positions at init time' but the system DOES have static edgePositions and vertexPositions from generateCubeGrowth() at init (fractalGrowthWireframe.ts lines 110-131). The same TS-side computeVibrantVertexColors approach used for the other 5 modes would work here, avoiding the unnecessary complexity of a GLSL-side tri-stop gradient function and uniform-based stop colors. The GLSL approach is over-engineered for this case.

wireframe.vert.glsl currently passes NO color varying to the fragment shader (only vFogFactor). All color is computed fragment-side from uPaletteHue. Steps 2-3 understate the wireframe shader changes needed: adding aVertexColor + vVertexColor varying to wireframe.vert.glsl is a larger change than for the other 5 modes which already have a vColor varying pipeline.

fractalGrowthWireframe uses TWO shader pairs (wireframe.vert/frag + wireframeVertex.vert/frag) — 4 shader files total. The plan mentions both frag files in Step 5 but doesn't clearly enumerate all 4 files that need modification, risking a missed shader.

The luminance cap formula in Step 6 (color *= min(1.0, 0.85 / max(lum, 0.001))) scales all RGB channels uniformly, which will desaturate vibrant colors toward grey when clamping activates. Consider a perceptual approach like clamping individual channels or using a soft-knee curve to preserve saturation.

The ticket title says 'all legacy modes' but the AC lists only 6 specific modes. The codebase has 4 additional systems (constellationLines, cubeLatticeWireframe, wireframePolyhedra, bezierCurveWeb) that may also be 'legacy modes'. The plan should clarify whether these are in or out of scope to avoid ambiguity.

Step 7 removes hsl2rgb from the 5 point-based vertex shaders but doesn't address whether wireframe.frag.glsl and wireframeVertex.frag.glsl still need hsl2rgb as a fallback (uHasVertexColor=0 path). If fractalGrowth is converted to TS-side vertex colors (per concern #1), the fallback path may be unnecessary, but this needs explicit decision.
